title: App icon in the viewer
question: Should the viewer show a project icon, where does it come from, and what happens when there isn't one?
rationale: Show a small app icon by the name — in the hero and beside the title — only when one's available, found without config (_project.json 'icon', then common filenames) and hidden if none load. Chosen over requiring a setting (friction) or no icon (loses identity); zero-config fits the calm spirit.

options.label: Auto-detect an icon (or _project.json "icon"); show in hero + name; hide if none

tradeoffs.note: the log looks like the project it belongs to
drop icon.png in the folder and it appears; or name it in _project.json
stays hidden entirely when no icon file loads
a small probe that tries a list of filenames and fills every icon slot

options.label: Only show an icon named explicitly in _project.json

tradeoffs.note: shows exactly what's configured, nothing inferred
have to edit JSON even to use an obvious icon.png sitting in the folder
every project's log looks the same
